_meta.title: FF Load
_meta.note: The real footage to matte. force_rate 0 PRESERVES the source fps (this is a transform, not generation) — the output frame_rate is wired from FF Video Info so fps round-trips automatically; buildMatAnyone patches force_rate + FF Output frame_rate only when an explicit fps param asks for a resample. frame_load_cap is always patched (defensive 30s cap). Accepts input-dir refs (jobs/<id>/clip.mp4) and annotated '<subfolder>/<file> [output]' paths for matting our own renders.

_meta.title: FF Video Info
_meta.note: Output 0 = fps (FLOAT) of the LOADED frames — feeds FF Output.frame_rate so the webm plays back at exactly the source rate. The pack's own example workflow ships this exact wiring (VHS_VideoInfoLoaded.fps -> VHS_VideoCombine.frame_rate).

_meta.title: FF Mask
_meta.note: User-supplied first-frame seed mask: WHITE = subject, BLACK = background. Only a SEED — MatAnyone2's warmup (n_warmup steps on the repeated first frame) regenerates a clean matte from it before propagating through the clip, so a rough brush-over is fine. OPTIONAL at the API level: when no mask is supplied, buildMatAnyone DELETES this node (and MatAnyone2's foreground_mask input) and adds 'FF First Frame' (ImageFromBatch, loaded frame 0) -> 'FF Auto Mask' (BiRefNetRMBG BiRefNet-general) whose MASK output feeds MatAnyone2.foreground_MASK instead — those two titles are forbidden in this file (validate-templates.mjs) so the builder-added nodes never collide.

_meta.title: FF MatAnyone
_meta.note: Video matting (pq-yang MatAnyone2 weights). foreground_mask takes the LoadImage IMAGE output directly (the node converts internally; no ImageToMask needed — matches the pack's example workflow). Its MASK-typed twin foreground_MASK (both optional in the live schema) is what the AUTO branch uses: buildMatAnyone wires BiRefNetRMBG's MASK output there when no seed mask is supplied. Outputs: 0 = matte (grayscale alpha as IMAGE, WHITE = subject — verified in run.py: green_screen = matte*src + (1-matte)*solid), 1 = green_screen composite (UNUSED here; solid_color is deliberately unwired, which makes output 1 an empty tensor at zero cost). mask_frame 0 = seed mask matches the first loaded frame. Memory knobs (max_internal_size/max_mem_frames/use_long_term) kept at pack defaults; raise use_long_term for very long clips.

_meta.title: MATTE_TO_MASK
_meta.note: The matte IMAGE is the alpha repeated to 3 identical channels; red carries it.

_meta.title: Invert For Join (JoinImageWithAlpha re-inverts)
_meta.note: Load-bearing, same discovery as wan_alpha.json: core JoinImageWithAlpha computes alpha = 1.0 - mask (SplitImageWithAlpha's inverse-mask convention). Matte WHITE = subject -> mask 1 at subject -> without this invert the subject would come out TRANSPARENT and the background opaque.

_meta.title: FF RGBA Join
_meta.note: Straight (non-premultiplied) alpha over the ORIGINAL source frames — MatAnyone2 has no foreground-color output, so soft edges keep the source's edge pixels.

_meta.title: FF Output
_meta.note: Same proven MG-ALPHA output idiom: VP9 webm with pix_fmt yuva420p so the alpha channel survives encoding (VHS webm format enum verified live: pix_fmt in [yuv420p, yuva420p]). AUDIO passthrough: the source clip's audio (FF Load output 2, already trimmed to the loaded frame window by VHS) is muxed via the webm format's libvorbis audio_pass — a silent source simply yields no audio track. frame_rate is LINKED from FF Video Info fps by default (source fps preserved); buildMatAnyone overwrites the link with a number only when the caller passes an explicit fps.
